title: Cross-session planning revision
preconditions: A learner has an active goal and trajectory.
Multiple learning sessions can be recorded against the same KC cluster.
Parent preferences require trajectory revision approval for guided autonomy.
setup_path: Run the seeded household setup through scripts/rehearse_proof_scenarios.py.
Use the parent-created Proof goal: equivalent fractions trajectory for Avery.
Record repeated weak/strong observations through the API rather than inserting audit rows manually.
execution_path: POST /api/content/generate and POST /api/learners/{student_id}/observations for multiple attempts against KC-FRAC-EQUIV.
GET /api/learners/{student_id}/workspace to refresh the active planning/session state.
GET /api/observability/adaptation/planning/{student_id} to inspect revision count, revisit density, scaffolding, and revision reasons.
GET /api/households/me/overview to see whether the parent posture blocks or exposes the revised plan.
visible_outcome: The active trajectory shows a new revision or increased revisit density with reasons tied to accumulated run summaries.
approval_hooks: Trajectory revision approval appears in the household overview when the guided parent policy sees a changed trajectory signature.

rehearsal_steps.action: Create a goal such as Grade 5 fraction equivalence.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: Planning state shows an active trajectory with checkpoints and current nodes.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: Dibble has a persistent plan beyond a single generated item.

rehearsal_steps.action: Record two or more sessions with weak outcomes, high support, and stalled mastery on the current node.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: Progression and adaptation summaries accumulate evidence across sessions.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: Trajectory decisions use durable evidence, not one-off response handling.

rehearsal_steps.action: Refresh active planning state after accumulated outcomes.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: Trajectory revision adjusts pacing, revisit density, bridge nodes, or scaffolding.
rehearsal_steps.proof_signal: The plan changes in response to learning evidence.

rehearsal_steps.action: Review any trajectory revision approval or summary.
rehearsal_steps.expected_observation: The parent sees why the trajectory changed and what happens next.

expected_observations: Trajectory history is inspectable and not overwritten silently.
Planning revision is attributable to evidence across sessions.
Parent approval posture is honored for trajectory changes.
success_criteria: The active trajectory changes after repeated weak outcomes.
The revision rationale references accumulated evidence.
Parent-facing review shows the changed next focus.
reset_notes: Use a fresh goal or learner when repeating this scenario to avoid prior trajectory history changing the baseline.
